task: Fix coolmax-oc.ps1 all errors + maximize everything for this PC. Combine with game220.ps1 all 28 nuclear helpers + Phase2 40 categories. Run and verify 3x until no doubt. Then apply GPU OC additions to all 221 game scripts (game1-game219, game220, gameauto). + [RESTORED] MegaToDo: Clone todoist-enhanced, rename to MegaToDo, setup database persistence, ensure working log + Extract top 300 longest profile functions to F:/study scripts (dot-source refactor)
context: MACHINE: RTX 5080 (nvml.dll, 3 fans, Fan0=read-only, Fan1+Fan2=physical), Gigabyte X870E AORUS PRO, AMD Ryzen CPU. PS v5 ONLY - no em-dash, semicolons not &&. KEY FILES: C:/Users/micha/Documents/WindowsPowerShell/coolmax-oc.ps1, C:/Users/micha/Documents/WindowsPowerShell/_SetGpuMaxOC.ps1, C:/Users/micha/Documents/WindowsPowerShell/_SetNvGpuFan.ps1. GAME DIR: F:/study/Dev_Toolchain/programming/C++/mingw-complete/mingw64/lib/gcc/x86_64-w64-mingw32/13.2.0/plugin/include/common/config/i386/FanAndGameControl/Game/ (game1-game220 + gameauto.ps1). game220.ps1 calls 28 nuclear helpers + _SetGamemaxPhase2 (40 categories). RTX 5080 Blackwell: NVML VF offsets NOT supported (err 3), use nvidia-smi -lgc/-lmc. Fan0=0% at idle (hw zero-stop), Fan1+Fan2 are real fans. NVML: SetPolicy MANUAL before SetSpeed, 6s ramp.

todos.text: [F:/study/WebBuilding/projects/MegaToDo] Read entire codebase (server.js, routes/, frontend/src/, netlify.toml) and identify all bugs, broken features, missing functionality - Mission: MegaToDo deploy and perfect
[F:/study/WebBuilding/projects/MegaToDo/server.js] Fix any backend bugs found: broken API routes, auth issues, database errors, missing endpoints - Mission: MegaToDo deploy and perfect
[F:/study/WebBuilding/projects/MegaToDo/frontend/src] Fix any frontend bugs: broken UI components, API call failures, routing issues, CSS/layout problems - Mission: MegaToDo deploy and perfect
[F:/study/WebBuilding/projects/MegaToDo/frontend] Run npm install and npm run build in frontend/ dir, verify build succeeds with zero errors - Mission: MegaToDo deploy and perfect
[F:/study/WebBuilding/projects/MegaToDo] Git add all changes, commit with descriptive message, push to origin main - Mission: MegaToDo deploy and perfect
[F:/study/WebBuilding/projects/MegaToDo] Deploy to Netlify via netlify deploy --prod, verify deployment URL is live - Mission: MegaToDo deploy and perfect
[Browser] Open deployed Netlify URL, test ALL features: signup, login, create/edit/delete tasks, filters, search, settings, responsive layout - Mission: MegaToDo deploy and perfect
[F:/study/WebBuilding/projects/MegaToDo] Fix ALL issues found during browser testing - every bug, UI glitch, broken feature - Mission: MegaToDo deploy and perfect
[F:/study/WebBuilding/projects/MegaToDo] Rebuild frontend, commit, push, redeploy to Netlify with all fixes - Mission: MegaToDo deploy and perfect
[Browser] Final comprehensive test of redeployed site: every page, every button, every feature must work flawlessly - confirm perfect deployment - Mission: MegaToDo deploy and perfect

[F:/study/WebBuilding/projects/MegaToDo] Add offline cache: service worker caches Todoist API responses in IndexedDB, shows cached tasks offline with Offline Mode banner, syncs on reconnect. VERIFY: disable DevTools network, reload, cached tasks visible with Offline Mode banner. -- Mission: MegaToDo deploy and perfect

[F:/study/WebBuilding/projects/MegaToDo] Add bulk operations: checkbox selection, bulk-complete/delete/move, Select All button, bulk action toolbar. VERIFY: select 3 tasks, bulk-complete, all 3 complete; undo restores them. -- Mission: MegaToDo deploy and perfect

[F:/study/WebBuilding/projects/MegaToDo] Add kanban board view: Today/This Week/Later/Someday columns, drag tasks between columns updates due date, toggle list vs kanban view. VERIFY: drag task from Today to This Week, due date updated in Todoist API. -- Mission: MegaToDo deploy and perfect

[F:/study/WebBuilding/projects/MegaToDo] Add time tracking: Start/Stop timer per task, entries in SQLite data/timelog.db, daily/weekly report view. VERIFY: start timer, wait 1 min, stop, 1 min logged in time report. -- Mission: MegaToDo deploy and perfect

[F:/study/WebBuilding/projects/MegaToDo] Add smart filter builder UI: build Todoist filter queries visually, save named filters, quick-access buttons. VERIFY: create Urgent Today = priority:1 AND due:today filter; save; clicking shows only matching tasks. -- Mission: MegaToDo deploy and perfect

[F:/study/WebBuilding/projects/MegaToDo] Fix OAuth for production domain: update Todoist developer app with Netlify redirect URI https://megatodo.netlify.app/oauth/callback; test full OAuth flow. VERIFY: login at megatodo.netlify.app completes OAuth; tasks load within 3s. -- Mission: MegaToDo deploy and perfect

[F:/study/WebBuilding/projects/MegaToDo/data] Set up SQLite with better-sqlite3: data/megatodo.db with tables tasks timelog filters. VERIFY: node -e require('./data/db.js') creates megatodo.db; .tables command shows 3 tables. -- Mission: MegaToDo deploy and perfect

[F:/study/WebBuilding/projects/MegaToDo] Add /done work log append: /done in task note appends timestamped entry to today Documentation Todoist task matching /done skill behavior. VERIFY: type /done in task note, entry appended with timestamp to Documentation project. -- Mission: MegaToDo deploy and perfect

[F:/study/WebBuilding/projects/MegaToDo] Add dark theme with purple accent #7c3aed: CSS custom properties, WCAG AA contrast >= 4.5:1 for all text elements. VERIFY: axe DevTools shows 0 contrast failures in dark mode. -- Mission: MegaToDo deploy and perfect

[F:/study/WebBuilding/projects/MegaToDo] Add keyboard shortcuts: T=Today W=Week K=Kanban /=search N=new task E=edit D=complete Del=delete Ctrl+S=save ?=cheat sheet overlay. VERIFY: press T switches to Today view; press N opens new task dialog. - - Mission: MegaToDo deploy and perfect
Force push the entire thing to the repository domain branch and then ewdeploy to netlify - Mission: MegaToDo deploy and perfect
test the deployed site To have everything as we expected it - Mission: MegaToDo deploy and perfect

missionExplanations.MegaToDo deploy and perfect:
GOAL: Clone the todoist-enhanced repository, rename it to MegaToDo, configure database persistence (replacing in-memory state), ensure the full Todoist API integration works with live task sync, and deploy a production-ready instance to Netlify or Heroku with persistent storage.

MegaToDo is a feature-rich Todoist client web app that adds bulk operations, time tracking, kanban view, and smart filtering on top of the standard Todoist API. Database persistence design: use a lightweight SQLite file stored in the repo's data/ folder (not gitignored) for custom fields and work log, while all core tasks stay in Todoist API as the source of truth. This avoids needing a database server.

Verification: create a task via the UI, verify it appears in Todoist app, append a work log entry, verify it persists on page refresh. GitHub: https://github.com/Michaelunkai/MegaToDo. Deployed: https://megatodo.netlify.app.

GOAL: Integrate OpenClaw (the multi-workspace Claude Code orchestrator) with a Mission Control dashboard that provides real-time visibility into all running workspaces, their RLP state, active todos, and resource usage -- and deploy it publicly so it is always accessible from any device.

Mission Control is a Next.js web application located at F:/Downloads/mission-control/mission-control/. It was built to give a unified command center for all Claude Code sessions running across 8 simultaneous workspaces (moltbot, openclaw, session2, main, etc.). Without it, monitoring requires manually switching between terminal tabs.

The OpenClaw integration adds agent-level visibility: each workspace Claude process is registered as an agent in Mission Control, showing its current status (idle, working, blocked), the last command it ran, and live stdout tail. Config for all 8 workspaces is in C:\Users\micha\.openclaw\. Each workspace inherits global commands from C:\Users\micha\.claude\commands\.

The RLP integration is the key new feature: a dedicated RLP QUEUE panel built as a React component (RlpPanel.tsx) that reads C:\Users\micha\.claude\workspace\rlp-state.json every 5 seconds via a Next.js /api/rlp-state route. It renders all missions with color-coded progress bars matching rlpl-runner.ps1 exactly: white=untouched, yellow=partial, blue=100% done. Clicking a mission expands its full todo list showing [ ] pending, [>] active, [x] done statuses. This gives a browser mirror of the terminal rlpl command, usable from phone or tablet.

A Server-Sent Events endpoint /api/rlp-watch uses fs.watch on rlp-state.json to push instant updates when any RLP session completes a todo. The rlp-chain.ps1 script POSTs to /api/rlp-notify after each completion for additional push path.

The deployment target is Netlify (NETLIFY_AUTH_TOKEN in F:\Downloads\ob\.env) via netlify deploy --prod. The Node.js server component runs as a Task Scheduler entry MissionControlServer using a VBS wrapper (SW_HIDE) to auto-start on logon.

GitHub: https://github.com/Michaelunkai/study2 (mission-control folder). Deployed: https://mission-control.netlify.app (URL confirmed after deployment).
